title: Stories behind Ozempic shaadis: ‘How did this mota guy get a girl like you?’
content: A growing number of Indians are turning to GLP-1 injections like Ozempic for rapid weight loss, primarily driven by wedding pressures and social media ideals. While these drugs are effective for obesity, medical professionals express concern over their cosmetic use, potential side effects, and the risk of counterfeit products. Experts warn against short-term use for quick results, emphasizing the need for medical supervision and a holistic approach to weight management.

title: Watch: Neymar breaks down in tears after Brazil's heartbreaking World Cup exit
content: Brazilian football legend Neymar has played his final international match after a tearful farewell following Brazil's World Cup exit. The 34-year-old, who debuted at the same New Jersey stadium where his career concluded, battled injury throughout the tournament. His departure marks the end of a decorated 16-year journey, leaving Brazil to usher in a new era.

title: Ram Temple Trust to hold key meeting amid donation row today: What's on agenda
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust convenes a critical meeting in Ayodhya amidst an embezzlement probe. Discussions will center on the resignations of key members Champat Rai and Anil Mishra, potential new administrative structures, and a briefing on the ongoing investigations into donation misuse. The trust will also review financial statements and consider appointing a CEO to oversee temple management.

title: AI will change the nature of jobs, create new ones: Citi CEO after tech layoffs
content: Citi CEO Jane Fraser revealed the bank is navigating two major AI races: integrating the technology into business models for revenue growth and adapting to its impact on jobs.  While acknowledging job dislocations and changes in job nature, Fraser highlighted AI's potential to augment human capabilities.  Citigroup recently laid off 3,500 tech roles in China as part of a broader global workforce reduction aimed at streamlining operations and boosting profitability.

title: 'Everyone should celebrate': USA coach hails Balogun red-card reprieve; Trump thanks FIFA
content: US coach Mauricio Pochettino has backed FIFA's controversial decision to lift Folarin Balogun's red card suspension, citing "ethics and integrity."  The striker will now be available for the World Cup clash against Belgium.  While President Trump praised the move, Belgium expressed astonishment and is considering legal action, questioning the precedent set by the ruling.

title: In a first, Hindu members included in Madhya Pradesh Wakf board after law amendment
content: The Madhya Pradesh government has reorganised the 10-member Wakf Board, with chief minister Mohan Yadav appointing Sanwar Patel as its chairman. The newly constituted board includes Manoj Malpani and Animesh Bhargava as Hindu members, making it the first state-level Wakf Board in the country to include non-Muslim members under the amended Act.

title: First 25 million US kids signing up for Trump Accounts to get donation from Dell couple
content: Dell Technologies founder and CEO Michael Dell and his wife Susan announced on July 4 that they'll give $250 each to the first 25 million qualifying American children who sign up for a Trump Account, the new government-backed investment vehicle that launched the same day. The pledge builds on the Dells' earlier $6.25 billion commitment. Here's how Trump Accounts work, who qualifies for the Dell donation, and what the money can eventually be used for.

title: 12-year-old ‘raped’, killed in Bengal’s South 24 Parganas, suspect lynched
content: A 12-year-old girl's body, found in a pond, sparked outrage in Baruipur, leading to violent protests. Locals lynched a suspect and clashed with police, damaging property. The Chief Minister has assured strict action and will meet the victim's father. A special investigation team is probing the murder and the subsequent unrest, with multiple FIRs lodged.

title: Hyderabad man leaves Amazon job to drive a taxi for a brave reason
content: A resident of Hyderabad has swapped his corporate role at Amazon for the freedom of full-time Uber driving, discovering true joy in engaging with diverse passengers and discovering his city. With over 23,600 rides under his belt since 2014, he emphasizes finding daily happiness rather than sticking to a conventional office job.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding: 20 wild details from NY ceremony
content: In a dazzling display of love, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce exchanged vows at Madison Square Garden, an event veiled in secrecy, complete with NDAs and a ban on phones. Officiated by Adam Sandler, the ceremony featured live performances by legends Paul McCartney and Stevie Nicks. Guests reveled in a whimsical 'Secret Garden' reception adorned with childhood mementos and bespoke gifts.

title: China's Agibot completes world's first livestream of humanoid robots inspecting full production line
content: Chinese firm Agibot has showcased the world's first live broadcast of humanoid robots on a tablet production line. Eight Agibot G2 robots worked non-stop for six days at Longcheer Technology, successfully inspecting and sorting 17,625 tablets with a near-perfect success rate. This demonstration highlights the practical application of embodied AI in real-world industrial settings, working alongside human employees and existing machinery.

title: FIFA WC: England script history, become first team to beat Mexico at Estadio Azteca
content: England achieved a historic 3-2 victory over Mexico at the Estadio Azteca, ending the hosts' unbeaten World Cup record at the iconic venue. Jude Bellingham's brace and Harry Kane's penalty secured the win despite Jarell Quansah's red card. England now advance to face Norway in the quarter-finals, while Mexico's Round of 16 exit streak continues.

title: Borrowed a car and died in crash? Family can't seek owner's insurance claim, says Delhi HC
content: A Delhi high court ruling clarifies that families cannot claim compensation from a car owner's personal accident insurance via the Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) if the borrower dies driving the vehicle. Such claims, particularly under Section 166 of the Motor Vehicles Act requiring proof of negligence, are deemed contractual and fall outside MACT's jurisdiction, necessitating pursuit in consumer forums or civil courts.

title: 'Restraining order needed': Trump's latest meme on Meloni ahead of Nato summit
content: Donald Trump reignited his public spat with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni by sharing a meme suggesting she needs a restraining order, just before their expected meeting at the Nato summit. This follows Trump's claims that Meloni sought photos and that Italy denied US runway access, allegations she strongly refuted as "completely made up." The exchange marks a significant downturn in their previously allied relationship.

title: Wedding, car, cash gifts: Ayodhya donation 'theft' accused reveals Rs 19 lakh spending trail
content: Fresh financial leads have emerged in the Ram Temple donation embezzlement case. Accused Avinash Shukla, during interrogation, reportedly detailed spending of Rs 19 lakh on family and friends, including wedding expenses for brothers and personal purchases like a car. Investigators are examining land deals and bank accounts, with legal action underway to recover funds. Valuables worth lakhs were previously recovered.

title: Katherine Heigl to say goodbye to 25-acre Utah mountain home as family begins new chapter
content: Actress Katherine Heigl and musician Josh Kelley are selling their sprawling 25-acre Utah mountain estate for $10.6 million after nearly two decades. The custom-built home, a hub for their family life and creative pursuits, is being left behind as they embrace a new chapter in a smaller, nearby Victorian farmhouse.

title: Harry Kane makes bizarre World Cup record, becomes first player since 1966 to...
content: Harry Kane achieved a unique FIFA World Cup record in England's thrilling 3-2 win over Mexico. The captain became the first player since 1966 to both score and concede a penalty in the same match. His penalty conversion marked his sixth goal of the tournament, drawing him level with Gerd Müller. Despite a late Mexican penalty, England held on to secure a quarter-final spot.

title: Harbhajan Singh watches 'Satluj' amid ban, praises Diljit Dosanjh
content: Former cricketer Harbhajan Singh lauded Diljit Dosanjh's film 'Satluj' for its powerful portrayal of human rights activist Jaswant Singh Khalra's fight for justice. The film, which highlights alleged illegal disappearances and secret cremations in Punjab, was recently removed from Indian OTT platforms. Harbhajan emphasized the importance of remembering Khalra's courage and the ongoing wait for answers for affected families.

title: Nato 3.0: Trump's dream, Putin's nightmare - the bloc's biggest reset since Cold War
content: The Nato Ankara Summit marks a pivotal moment as the alliance shifts gears from US dependency towards a robust, European-led defence coalition. Motivated by an unpredictable America and an assertive Russia, Nato is emphasizing agility, industrial capabilities, and strong partnerships. This meeting will challenge Europe to strengthen its defense posture and readiness in response to the Ukraine crisis, which is vital for establishing a credible deterrent.

title: Was Delta flight struck by July 4 fireworks in Chicago? Pilot says 'felt a big bang'
content: A Delta flight landing in Chicago on July 4th reported a startling 'big bang' as it was apparently struck by fireworks at a low altitude. Air traffic control acknowledged multiple similar incidents, even warning another pilot of a 'war zone.' While the Delta flight landed safely with no injuries and no damage found, the FAA has launched an investigation into the dangerous close calls during the holiday celebrations.

title: FIFA WC: Bellingham scores twice in 98 seconds as England beat Mexico to reach QF
content: England triumphed 3-2 against Mexico at the Estadio Azteca, overcoming a hostile crowd and a second-half red card to reach the FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-finals. Jude Bellingham's brace and a Harry Kane penalty secured the win despite playing with 10 men. The Three Lions now face Norway next, aiming for a semi-final spot after a dramatic encounter.

title: 'Ego still riding high': Delhi CM rebuts Kejriwal over 'who are you' attack on Nabin
content: Delhi chief minister Rekha Gupta, accused the former Delhi chief minister of allowing political frustration to turn into arrogance. In a post on X, Gupta first introduced Nabin while highlighting his political journey and credentials. She then launched a direct attack on Kejriwal, saying: "Everyone knows you're feeling desperate and disheartened. But it seems your ego is still riding high in the seventh heaven!" she said.

title: Re-Neet 2026 Result: NTA to release OMR sheets, final key by July 20
content: The National Testing Agency is expected to release the Re-NEET 2026 OMR response sheets, final answer key and result soon on the official website. Candidates can download the OMR sheets using their login credentials and verify recorded responses. The result will be prepared based on the final answer key after objections are reviewed. The scorecard will include key candidate details, ranks and qualifying status, while expected qualifying marks and admission cutoffs indicate likely trends for counselling.

title: Haaland, Messi and Mbappé script unprecedented FIFA World Cup history
content: A historic scoring milestone has been reached at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with Erling Haaland, Lionel Messi, and Kylian Mbappé all netting seven goals each. Haaland's late brace secured Norway's stunning upset over Brazil, propelling them to the quarterfinals. This unprecedented feat has intensified the Golden Boot race, showcasing a captivating battle between football's modern greats.

title: Newlywed dies after 'fall' from building in Delhi; family alleges dowry harassment
content: A 28-year-old newlywed woman tragically died after an alleged fall from a residential building in South Delhi. Her family claims foul play, citing dowry harassment and physical abuse by her husband. They assert she didn't reside in the building where she was found. Police are investigating all possibilities, including suicide, and verifying the family's allegations of dowry demands and torture.

title: Neymar retires from international football after Brazil's World Cup exit to Norway
content: Brazil’s World Cup 2026 campaign ended in a shock 2-1 Round of 16 defeat to Norway at MetLife Stadium, marking a heartbreaking farewell for Neymar, who confirmed his international retirement after scoring a stoppage-time penalty. The 34-year-old leaves as Brazil’s all-time leading scorer with 80 goals, closing a 16-year career spanning four World Cups and Olympic glory.

title: 5 injured after powerful explosion at retired Army Subedar's house in Rewari
content: A powerful explosion rocked Hans Nagar, Rewari, injuring at least five people at the residence of a retired Army Subedar. The blast was so intense that locals described it feeling like an earthquake. Four of the injured have been moved to a higher medical facility in Rohtak for advanced treatment. Police and forensic teams are investigating the cause of the severe incident, with significant damage reported inside the house.

title: Syama Prasad Mookerjee devoted his life to India's unity and progress: PM Modi
content: Remembering Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee on his 125th birth anniversary, this tribute highlights his unwavering commitment to India's unity and selfless service. From his early life of privilege to his impactful roles as an educationist, minister, and founder of the Jana Sangh, Mookerjee championed national integrity and democratic values. His sacrifice for Jammu & Kashmir's integration remains a pivotal moment in India's history.

title: Monsoon mayhem: South Bombay sees wettest July; house collapse, tree falls claim 8 lives
content: Mumbai experienced a devastating deluge, with record-breaking rainfall causing a building collapse in Mankhurd that claimed six lives, including five children. Unusually strong winds uprooted trees, leading to further fatalities. Flight operations were disrupted as the city recorded its wettest July day in over 50 years, with more heavy rain anticipated and a holiday declared for educational institutions.

title: Bank holiday today: Where are banks shut on July 6? Check state-wise list
content: Plan your bank visits carefully as several branches will be closed on specific dates this July due to regional holidays. West Bengal and Mizoram observe closures today, July 6. Further state-specific holidays are scheduled across Meghalaya, Odisha, Uttarakhand, Manipur, Sikkim, and Tripura. Remember, Sundays and the second and fourth Saturdays also mean branch closures. Digital banking services remain unaffected.

title: Russia launches fresh missile, drone attack on Kyiv ahead of Nato summit in Turkey
content: Kyiv Mayor Vitaliy Klitschko said debris from intercepted missiles and drones caused fires and damage in at least two districts of the capital. Air raid sirens had sounded across the city shortly before the explosions. The latest bombardment came a day after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that Russia was preparing another large-scale attack.

title: 'Have they house arrested her?' TMC after forces deployed outside Mamata's home
content: Security forces surrounded Mamata Banerjee's residence as she prepared to visit Baruipur following a horrific crime. The TMC condemned the deployment, calling it a "super emergency" and questioning if their leader was being placed under house arrest. The party also criticized the BJP, alleging their promises of women's safety have proven hollow amidst the ongoing tragedy.

title: NFL trade rumors: Chiefs, ravens linked to $12.3M Panthers WR
content: Carolina Panthers' first-round pick Xavier Legette is generating trade buzz, with the Kansas City Chiefs and Baltimore Ravens emerging as potential destinations. Following the arrivals of new receivers, Legette's role in Carolina has diminished. Both the Chiefs and Ravens could benefit from his size, speed, and affordable contract, offering him a fresh start in offenses led by Patrick Mahomes and Lamar Jackson respectively.

title: 'I will finish when I decide': Ronaldo shuts down retirement speculation
content: Cristiano Ronaldo has confirmed the FIFA World Cup 2026 will be the final World Cup of his legendary career, saying he will retire from football’s biggest stage “with a clear conscience” after giving everything for Portugal. The 41-year-old made the announcement ahead of Portugal’s Round of 16 clash with Spain, insisting his focus remains on extending his farewell by reaching the quarter-finals.

title: Jaishankar meets Qatar PM, lauds mediation efforts between US and Iran
content: During his visit, Jaishankar met Qatari prime minister and minister of foreign affairs, Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani and "reviewed the entire spectrum of bilateral relations with a focus on energy, trade, investments and people to people ties. They also discussed regional and multilateral issues of mutual interest". The technical talks are expected to take place on July 11, although a final decision on the venue has yet to be made.

title: Isro tests new motor for rocket being built for Gaganyaan parachute trials
content: India's space agency, Isro, has successfully tested a crucial solid motor for a new sub-orbital vehicle supporting the Gaganyaan human spaceflight program. This vehicle will test the crew module's parachute system by lofting it to high altitudes. Modified from a PSLV motor, this test marks a significant step towards safely returning astronauts from space.

title: 'I challenge the darkness': Diljit Dosanjh reacts after 'Satluj' OTT removal
content: Diljit Dosanjh's film 'Satluj', previously facing CBFC issues, was removed from Zee5 in India just two days after its digital release. The actor reacted strongly, posting '#ichallengethedarkness' and stating the film's unavailability is a setback. The streaming platform expressed commitment to bringing the film back, emphasizing its authentic narrative, while acknowledging the pause in its availability.

title: Trump intervention forces stunning FIFA U-turn as Balogun escapes World Cup ban
content: FIFA's recent decision to rescind Folarin Balogun's red card has sparked controversy, enabling the US talent to compete against Belgium. This unusual action came after US President Donald Trump personally urged FIFA President Gianni Infantino. Belgium has expressed outrage and is seeking legal recourse. Meanwhile, the US coach supported the outcome, emphasizing the previous challenges faced by his team. Balogun's participation revives American aspirations for the quarter-finals.

title: With 2027 in mind, BWSSB tells citizens to install aerators, use drinking water judiciously
content: Given a severely deficit monsoon this year, with water levels in reservoirs already running low, the summer of 2027 is expected to be parched

title: Mumbai rains | Pune–Mumbai Expressway, old Mumbai–Pune Highway shut due following landslide, heavy rainfall
content: The traffic police have issued a public appeal, requesting that citizens refrain from undertaking any journeys between Pune and Mumbai until further notice

title: Visit to Indonesia, Australia, New Zealand will further strengthen India's Act East Policy: PM
content: ‘My visit to Indonesia and Australia in the Eastern and Southern Indian Ocean, respectively, followed by New Zealand, will further strengthen India's Act East Policy’, said Prime Minister Narendra Modi

title: Kerala man arrested for cultivating cannabis plants outside flat
content: After a tip-off, police discovered 22 cannabis plants in polythene bags in front of the accused’s flat in Kollam. Investigative officers said the suspect grew and dried the marijuana with the clear intention of selling it

title: How are terrorists designated under the UAPA? | Explained
content: What is the law under which terrorist designations taken place? Who were the first individuals to be designated terrorists? What is the process involved?

title: Two, including 8-year-old girl, injured in firing by armed groups in Manipur’s Kangpokpi
content: The injured were initially provided first aid at an Assam Rifles camp and later brought to the Regional Institute of Medical Sciences in Imphal for further treatment

title: NMC rejects proposals for new government medical colleges in Ramanagara, Kanakapura and Bagalkote
content: The main reasons cited are the non-appointment of necessary human resources, including teaching and non-teaching faculties and non-development of other infrastructure, including buildings

title: Five children among 6 killed, in Mumbai chawl collapse
content: According to the information given by Rajawadi Hospital, a seven-year-old boy died during treatment, while the condition of a 24-year-old man is stable

title: Crucial Ram temple trust meeting today; treasurer distances himself from donation row
content: Ahead of the meeting, Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust treasurer Govind Dev Giri distanced himself from the controversy, saying he had no role in the daily donation process

title: Eastern India lead in GI registrations, MP records highest registration in 2025-26
content: The DPIIT Chair of WBNUJS and JHARCRAFT have secured maximum number of GI registrations for West Bengal and Jharkhand respectively.

title: Mumbai-Pune train services suspended after landslides in Karjat-Lonavala section
content: The disruptions led to the cancellation of several trains, including the CSMT-Pune Indrayani Express, Intercity Express, Deccan Express, Deccan Queen, Pragati Express and Dhule Express

title: 316 students graduate at IIIT-B convocation, highest offer stands at ₹1.5 crore
content: 316 students celebrated their graduation during the 26th convocation of the International Institute of Information Technology Bangalore (IIIT-B).

title: Iran’s move to invite Sunni Kashmir leader Mehbooba conveys a message
content: Mehbooba Mufti attending supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s final rites is bound to change the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) president’s status in politics now onwards

title: 20 lakh names deleted after SIR Phase I in Odisha
content: BJD challenges draft report, saying 27 lakh voters were deleted from electoral roll as against the claimed exclusion of 20 Lakh

title: Manipur SIR: 19.34 lakh voters in draft electoral roll, says CEO
content: The CEO said 1,08,283 voters had either "shifted, were absent as the BLOs did not find these electors or did not get back their filled enumeration forms before June 28

title: Monsoon disrupts lives across India; Mumbai air traffic hit, Himachal, Kerala, Odisha on alert | Updates on July 5
content: Amid threat of heavy rains, Mumbai civic body orders schools and colleges to remain shut on July 6, 2026

title: India needs to understand conditions better: Kishan
content: The wicketkeeper-batter lauds Bethell’s match-winning knock but adds that the three no-balls by leg-spinner Bishnoi made things easy for the young English southpaw in the second T20I

title: The missing link in India’s maternal health story
content: Despite improvements in institutional deliveries, women’s empowerment and maternity programmes, exclusive breastfeeding rates in India have declined. NFHS-6 data point to persistent gaps in postnatal support, workplace protection and maternity benefits. Bindu Shajan Perappadan looks at medical, social and economic factors contributing to the decline

title: Government issues stern notice to Meta on child sexual abuse material in Instagram ads: sources
content: “MeitY has ordered Instagram to disable all ads and content promoting and facilitating access to CSEAM,” the sources said, adding that the Ministry also demanded a detailed explanation within seven days

title: Long-overdue digital survey for Thammanam–Pullepady Road project gets under way
content: Project aims to construct a 3.7-km-long four-lane road linking MG Road with the Edappally–Vyttila NH 66 Bypass by widening and extending the Thammanam–Pullepady Road

title: 256 criminal cases pending against lawmakers of Kerala
content: Twenty-two cases against lawmakers delayed for more than two years and nine cases for over five years

title: AI-powered tools can trace missing persons faster, with more accuracy, say experts
content: They can significantly improve speed and accuracy of investigations, especially when time is critical

title: Four persons held for stealing copper wires at railway yard
content: Railway officials found that copper wires on signalling systems along the track between Arakkonam and Tiruttani and Arakkonam and Katpadi were missing for the past several weeks

title: Police to launch screening of rental properties in Kozhikode under Operation Toofan
content: Move follows intelligence inputs that drug traffickers and repeat offenders are using rented premises to store smuggled narcotics; database of landlords and rental properties in vulnerable areas to be prepared as part of the drive

title: State Highways opens ₹20.81-crore ROB in Tirupattur
content: Originally given its nod in 2020, the new bridge was jointly done by the Southern Railway and the Projects wing of the State Highways

title: Six persons arrested for assaulting man at Tiruvannamalai GH
content: The police said when the ambulance halted at the hospital, the gang dragged an injured Silambarasan from the vehicle and beat him before police rescued him

title: Police seize 3.6 kg of ganja in Villupuram, two held
content: The police said initial inquiry revealed the duo had bought ganja from drug peddlers to sell it to individuals in Villupuram district

title: Heavy Mumbai rains: Three dead, four injured as rain disrupts life in Maharashtra
content: Flight operations affected for an hour at Mumbai airport; holiday declared for schools and colleges in Mumbai and Thane on July 5

title: The real crisis in India’s fisheries
content: Official claims that most of India’s marine fish stocks are sustainable should not distract from the continuing degradation of the country’s inshore fishing grounds; stronger governance of coastal waters and better management of mechanised trawling are essential for truly sustainable fisheries

title: Over 230 Irula families in Ariyalur find steady income through cashew harvesting initiative
content: The Tribal Welfare Department facilitated financial support for people from the Irula community to participate in public auctions for cashew plantations managed by the Tamil Nadu Forest Plantation Corporation Limited to enable them to directly participate in cashew cultivation and marketing

title: Policeman from Kangpokpi questioned in abduction and killing of six Naga men in Manipur
content: Following public outrage over the killings, the State Government handed over the case to the National Investigation Agency, which is yet to make any arrests

title: Amit Shah to launch several projects as Ministry of Cooperation completes five years
content: He will lay the foundation stone for 47 grain storage godowns and launch a centralised core banking platform, and Sahakar Sahyogi, a conversational AI-powered platform for Urban Cooperative Banks

title: BJP chief hits out at SP, Congress over Ram Temple issue
content: No individual would be allowed to hurt the sentiments of devotees, says Nitin Nabin, without explicitly referring to the recent allegations of financial irregularities at the Ram Temple

title: SIT formed to probe ₹100-crore disproportionate assets case against former TMC councillor
content: Police arrested Debraj Chakraborty, husband of former Rajarhat-Gopalpur MLA Aditi Munshi, on Wednesday based on an FIR filed by BJP MLA Tarunjyoti Tewari against the couple

title: Gujarat police reunite missing Maharashtra woman with family
content: The woman, who is mentally ill, was found hundreds of kilometres away from her home; she is among the 314 adults and 100 children traced and reunited with their families by the Amreli police since 2025

title: Former SNDP Yogam leader Mahesan’s family meets Home Minister, seeks fresh probe into his death
content: The family urged the Minister to intensify legal action against the first accused, SNDP Yogam general secretary Vellappally Natesan, and constitute a Special Investigation Team (SIT) to reinvestigate the case

title: We are prepared for 2-D Bills — Delimitation and Dismissal: Jairam Ramesh
content: Congress general secretary (communications) Jairam Ramesh claims that the government does not have the numbers to pass either the Delimitation Bill or the Constitution (130th Amendment) Bill for removal of Ministers

title: First instalment of ₹25,863 crore under VB-GRAM G released
content: Uttar Pradesh received the highest allocation at ₹3,210.76 crore, followed by Andhra Pradesh, Rajasthan, and Tamil Nadu, while Kerala received a considerably smaller allocation of ₹925.33 crore; concerns over centralised planning and high technological barrier persist

title: Pothundy twin murder verdict on July 6
content: Lone accused, Chenthamara of Thiruthampadam, Nenmara, is already serving double life imprisonment for the murder of his neighbour Sajitha, who was hacked to death on August 31, 2019

title: Row over Meghalaya tribal council’s Blinkit entry ban
content: A traditional local body criticises the Khasi Hills Autonomous District Council for denying doorstep delivery service of discounted products to six lakh people to protect the interests of 4,000 shops in and around Shillong

title: Complaint by actor Ansiba Hassan: Court directs registration of FIR against Lakshmipriya, SI
content: Ansiba has accused Lakshmipriya of filing a false complaint against her, and Reshma, SI of women’s cell, of subjecting her to harassment and unlawful detention in the name of ‘verifying’  allegations against her

title: Foundation laid for Adani Group’s defence factory in M.P.’s Shivpuri
content: A missile and propellant manufacturing facility is being set up by the Adani Defence and Aerospace at a cost of ₹2,500 crore

title: Oil price today: Crude slips after OPEC+ announces raising output targets from August
content: the TOI Business Desk is a vigilant and dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant business news from around the world to readers of the Times of India . the desk ensures that TOI readers are well-informed about the ever-changing and dynamic world of commerce .

title: Trump Shares Fake Obama Image With Arabic, 'Black Lives Matter' Graffiti
content: President Donald Trump has a yearslong record of intensely personal criticism of the Obamas, and of using incendiary, sometimes racist, rhetoric.

title: Super Typhoon Bavi Makes Landfall In US Pacific Island, Residents Warned To Stay Indoors
content: Super Typhoon Bavi hit the Northern Mariana Islands with 100+ mph winds, forcing residents indoors as officials warn of life-threatening danger

title: Ballroom, Kennedy Center, Oval Office: Inside Trump's White House Makeover
content: Trump is reshaping Washington with a new ballroom, a 250-foot arch, and Kennedy Center renovations sparking lawsuits, backlash, and controversy

title: UK Tightens Rules On Overseas Donations Amid Farage Funding Probe
content: UK tightens overseas political donation rules to curb foreign interference, as Farage faces a watchdog probe over an undeclared crypto donation.

title: Israeli Votes To Defy Supreme Court Ruling On Broadcast Regulator
content: This is the first time Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government has flouted a Supreme Court ruling, although it has clashed with the judiciary in the past.

title: 'Need India's Help': PoK Leader's Cry For Aid Amid Pakistan's Brutalities
content: The appeal comes as PoK witnesses large-scale protests against the Pakistani administration since last month. Last week, during a massive protest rally held at the Eidgah Ground, people chanted slogans like "PoK is not part of Pakistan".

title: Traffic Suspended On Pune-Mumbai Highway After Flooding, Landslide
content: The authorities appealed to citizens to avoid travelling between Pune and Mumbai until further orders.

title: Weather Today LIVE: Heavy Rain Grounds Flights, Triggers Flood Alerts Across India
content: Delhi-NCR Monsoon Alert Live Updates: The disruptions occurred amid the ongoing monsoon season, when periods of intense rainfall and gusty winds frequently impact air travel, particularly on high-density routes such as the Delhi-Mumbai corridor.

title: Man Takes Wife To Girlfriend's Room, Shoots Her Dead. Couple Flees To Nepal
content: In Gurugram's Manesar, a 25-year-old man allegedly shot dead his wife just three months after their wedding with the help of his girlfriend

title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei Funeral News Updates: Iran Begins Ali Khamenei's Funeral Procession through Tehran
content: Ali Khamenei's Last Rites News Live: Authorities have shut down streets, airspace and daily life for the mourning, which will end on Thursday as the 86-year-old Khamenei is buried at the Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad, his birthplace

title: 'Kill Trump' Calls At Ali Khamenei's Funeral As Top Leaders Reemerge From Hiding
content: Posters and graffiti at the Grand Mosalla even called for the assassination of Trump and Netanyahu, with similar slogans echoing the crowd.

title: 'Death To America, Death To Israel' Chants Echo At Khamenei's Funeral In Iran
content: The comment represents the first, direct call for Trump's death by an emcee at the funeral, which has seen posters and graffiti calling for the killing of Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

title: Cities Are Turning Into Heat Traps. Climate Change Isn't The Only Reason
content: The "Urban Heat Island" effect -- an invisible phenomenon that is quietly making India's cities several degrees hotter than their surroundings.

title: 'Shocked, Saddened': Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj Ban Triggers Political Row
content: The Shiromani Gurdwara Parbandhak Committee (SGPC), the body that manages prominent gurdwaras in the country, and the Shiromani Akali Dal have thrown their weight behind the movie.

title: Zelesky Says Fight Continuing In Key Town That Kremlin Says It Seized
content: Moscow earlier this week announced it had taken the key eastern town of Kostyantynivka but Kyiv has denied that as a "lie", saying that it is defending the town.

title: JD Vance's US "Only Ally" Remark, Netanyahu's "1.4 Billion Indians" Reply
content: Israeli Prime Minister Bejamin Netanyahu has pushed back against JD Vance's comments, saying that his country gets "tremendous" support from India.

title: Category 5 Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall, hits US Pacific islands with destructive winds
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers.
